page: Vultr vcg-h100-216c-1914gb-640vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server configured with an Intel x86_64 architecture featuring 216 vCPUs and 1884.0 GB of system memory. The hardware profile includes a massive local storage array consisting of 13312 GB of NVMe SSD storage, alongside a single complimentary public IPv4 address.

The primary differentiator of this server is its integrated GPU acceleration, featuring eight NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs. This configuration delivers a total of 640 GB of high-bandwidth video memory (VRAM), with each GPU providing 80 GB of dedicated VRAM. This hardware layout is engineered to support intensive parallel processing tasks.

From a resource density perspective, the server offers a high ratio of GPU and storage resources relative to the system memory and CPU cores. While the CPU allocation is designated as shared, the sheer scale of the 216 vCPUs and the dedicated nature of the eight H100 GPUs position this server as a high-density resource allocation. The qualitative cost efficiency is centered on the consolidation of massive parallel computing power and multi-terabyte NVMe storage within a single bare-metal footprint, reducing the need for multi-node cluster orchestration for large workloads.

This server configuration is designed for workloads requiring extreme parallel computation and large memory footprints. Suitable use cases include large language model training and inference, deep learning, complex data science simulations, high-throughput data processing pipelines, and massive database hosting.
description: Vultr vcg-h100-216c-1914gb-640vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server designed for high-density parallel computing. It features an Intel x86_64 architecture with 216 vCPUs and 1884.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 8.72 GB of RAM per core. The server is equipped with eight NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs, providing a combined total of 640 GB of VRAM. Storage is supported by a 13312 GB local NVMe SSD array. This hardware profile is optimized for massive parallel processing tasks, such as large language model training, deep learning, and complex data science simulations. The consolidation of eight enterprise-grade GPUs and multi-terabyte NVMe storage within a single bare-metal instance provides a highly efficient environment for resource-intensive workloads, eliminating the network overhead of multi-node clustering.
og_description: Vultr vcg-h100-216c-1914gb-640vram is a Dedicated Metal GPU server featuring 216 Intel vCPUs, 1884 GB RAM, 13.3 TB NVMe SSD storage, and eight NVIDIA H100 GPUs with 640 GB total VRAM.

tagline: A high-density bare-metal server featuring eight NVIDIA H100 GPUs, 1.8 TB of RAM, and 13.3 TB of NVMe storage for parallel computing.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs with 640 GB total VRAM
216 Intel x86_64 vCPUs with 1884.0 GB system memory
13312 GB of local NVMe SSD storage

Optimized for deep learning, LLM training, and data science
